message:
feat(fuzz): add fuzz_dump_restore + tighten RESP parser fuzz configs

Adds a structure-aware DUMP/RESTORE fuzz target and tightens the
existing RESP parser fuzz configs to exercise narrow-depth edge cases
that the default config misses.

fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_dump_restore.rs (new)
  * Structure-aware input via `arbitrary::Arbitrary`:
      - `DumpRestoreInput::Valid(ValidDumpRestoreCase)` — constructs
        a legitimate key/TTL/value triple using a `DumpValue` enum
        that covers String, List, Set, Hash, SortedSet, Stream
        payloads. Exercises the full DUMP → RESTORE → GET round-trip
        against a real `fr_store::Store`.
      - `DumpRestoreInput::Raw(RawRestoreCase)` — slings arbitrary
        bytes at RESTORE with a trailing sentinel, covering the
        defensive "hostile payload" path where a caller hands us
        bytes that never came out of DUMP. Target is that we reject
        cleanly (StoreError::Restore variants) without panicking.
  * Bounds: MAX_KEY_LEN=32, MAX_BLOB_LEN=64, MAX_COLLECTION_LEN=8,
    MAX_STREAM_ENTRIES=6, MAX_STREAM_FIELDS=6,
    MAX_RAW_PAYLOAD_LEN=2_048; libfuzzer wrapper caps input length
    at 4096 so the per-iteration cost stays low.
  * NOW_MS=1_000 anchors all TTL math deterministically.

fuzz/corpus/fuzz_dump_restore/ (new)
  * `empty` (1 byte) and `minimal` (5 bytes) starter seeds so the
    target starts with libfuzzer coverage from iteration one.

fuzz/Cargo.toml
  * Add `[dependencies.fr-store]` path dep (the new target's oracle
    lives in fr-store).
  * Register the new `[[bin]] fuzz_dump_restore` target.

fuzz/README.md
  * Document `fuzz_dump_restore` in the target table under
    "DUMP/RESTORE payload handling — Structure-aware round-trip +
    hostile payload invariants" and add a copy-pasteable
    `cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_dump_restore` recipe.

fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_resp_parser.rs
  * Drop the `..ParserConfig::default()` tail spread from the
    restrictive and lenient configs: with all fields explicitly
    named (max_bulk_len=512, max_array_len=16,
    max_recursion_depth=4), the spread tail masked future
    field additions — removing it forces an explicit choice.

fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_resp_roundtrip.rs
  * Same `..ParserConfig::default()` removal on the round-trip
    config so additions to ParserConfig are caught at compile time
    rather than silently inheriting defaults.

Related bead: frankenredis-0iyb (fuzz targets for RESP/AOF/RDB
parsers) — the DUMP/RESTORE target closes the remaining parser-
surface gap flagged in that epic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -19,6 +19,9 @@ path = "../crates/fr-protocol"
 [dependencies.fr-persist]
 path = "../crates/fr-persist"
 
+[dependencies.fr-store]
+path = "../crates/fr-store"
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_resp_parser"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_resp_parser.rs"
@@ -46,3 +49,10 @@ path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_resp_roundtrip.rs"
 test = false
 doc = false
 bench = false
+
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_dump_restore"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_dump_restore.rs"
+test = false
+doc = false
+bench = false

@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@ This directory contains fuzz targets for security-critical parser surfaces.
 | `fuzz_resp_roundtrip` | RESP encode/decode | Round-trip invariant |
 | `fuzz_aof_decoder` | AOF file parsing | Crash detector |
 | `fuzz_rdb_decoder` | RDB file parsing | Crash detector |
+| `fuzz_dump_restore` | DUMP/RESTORE payload handling | Structure-aware round-trip + hostile payload invariants |
 
 ## Running Fuzz Tests
 
@@ -26,6 +27,9 @@ cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_aof_decoder
 # Run RDB decoder fuzzer
 cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_rdb_decoder
 
+# Run DUMP/RESTORE fuzzer
+cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_dump_restore
+
 # Run round-trip invariant checker
 cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_resp_roundtrip
 ```
